instruction: Create a health system Blueprint with 200 max health. It should take 20 damage when hit and destroy the actor when health reaches zero.

BLUEPRINT: BP_HealthSystem
PARENT: Actor
CATEGORY: Gameplay

VAR MaxHealth: Float = 200

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="TakeDamage"]
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=CurrentHealth]
NODE n3: SubtractFloat [B=20]
NODE n4: ClampFloat [Min=0.0, Max=200]
NODE n5: SetVar [Variable=CurrentHealth]
NODE n6: LessEqualFloat [B=0.0]
NODE n7: Branch
NODE n8: DestroyActor
NODE n9: PrintString [InString="Damage Taken"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.True -> n8.Execute
EXEC n7.False -> n9.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.Value [Float]
DATA n5.Value -> n6.A [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Condition [Bool]

NODE n4: Event_ActorEndOverlap

NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Door Closing"]

EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute

instruction: Create a destructible actor with Health=100. On AnyDamage subtract the damage from Health. If Health drops to zero or below, print Destroyed, play a sound, and destroy. Otherwise print the remaining health
output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Destructible
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n3: SubtractFloat
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n5: LessThanOrEqual [B=0.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Destroyed!"]
NODE n8: PlaySound2D
NODE n9: DestroyActor
NODE n10: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute
EXEC n6.False -> n10.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n10.InString [String]

EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute

DATA n10.ReturnValue -> n12.A [Float]
DATA n12.ReturnValue -> n13.Condition [Bool]

instruction: Create a day night controller. Timer variable increments by DeltaSeconds on Tick. When Timer exceeds 60, reset Timer to 0, toggle IsNight using FlipFlop, and print either Night Falls or Dawn Breaks
output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DayNight
PARENT: Actor

VAR Timer: Float = 0.0
VAR IsNight: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Timer]
NODE n3: AddFloat
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Timer]
NODE n5: GreaterThan [B=60.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: SetVar [Variable=Timer, Value=0.0]
NODE n8: FlipFlop
NODE n9: SetVar [Variable=IsNight, Value=true]
NODE n10: PrintString [InString="Night Falls"]
NODE n11: SetVar [Variable=IsNight, Value=false]
NODE n12: PrintString [InString="Dawn Breaks"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.A -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.Then -> n10.Execute
EXEC n8.B -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Then -> n12.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]

instruction: Create a moving platform. On BeginPlay print Platform Active. Use a looping timer that alternates direction with FlipFlop. On A, move up 300 units and print Going Up. On B, move down 300 units and print Going Down
output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_MovingPlatform
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="Platform Active"]
NODE n3: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="MovePlatform", Time=3.0, Looping=true]

NODE n5: FlipFlop
NODE n6: AddActorLocalOffset [DeltaLocation={X=0,Y=0,Z=300}]
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Going Up"]
NODE n8: AddActorLocalOffset [DeltaLocation={X=0,Y=0,Z=-300}]
NODE n9: PrintString [InString="Going Down"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.A -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n5.B -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute

instruction: Create a complete wave spawner. On BeginPlay, print Wave 1 Starting and start a 15 second looping timer called SpawnWave. Each time SpawnWave fires: increment WaveNumber, print the wave number, spawn enemies equal to WaveNumber times 2 by printing Spawning X enemies, then check if WaveNumber exceeds MaxWaves (default 10). If exceeded, print Victory All Waves Complete, clear the timer. Track EnemiesThisWave variable
output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_WaveSpawner
PARENT: Actor

VAR WaveNumber: Int = 0
VAR MaxWaves: Int = 10
VAR EnemiesThisWave: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="Wave 1 Starting"]
NODE n3: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="SpawnWave", Time=15.0, Looping=true]
NODE n4: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="SpawnWave"]
NODE n5: GetVar [Variable=WaveNumber]
NODE n6: AddFloat [B=1]
NODE n7: SetVar [Variable=WaveNumber]
NODE n8: PrintString
NODE n9: MultiplyFloat [B=2]
NODE n10: SetVar [Variable=EnemiesThisWave]
NODE n11: PrintString
NODE n12: GetVar [Variable=MaxWaves]
NODE n13: GreaterThan
NODE n14: Branch
NODE n15: PrintString [InString="Victory! All Waves Complete!"]
NODE n16: ClearTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="SpawnWave"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n10.Execute
EXEC n10.Then -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Then -> n14.Execute
EXEC n14.True -> n15.Execute
EXEC n15.Then -> n16.Execute

DATA n5.Value -> n6.A [Int]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Value [Int]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n8.InString [String]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n9.A [Int]
DATA n9.ReturnValue -> n10.Value [Int]
DATA n9.ReturnValue -> n11.InString [String]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n13.A [Int]
DATA n12.Value -> n13.B [Int]
DATA n13.ReturnValue -> n14.Condition [Bool]

instruction: Create a wave manager using a different approach. It has WaveCount starting at 1 and a SpawnDelay that starts at 10 and decreases by 1 each wave (minimum 3). On BeginPlay start the first wave. The DoWave custom event prints the wave, spawns enemies, waits SpawnDelay seconds using Delay, increments WaveCount, reduces SpawnDelay, then calls DoWave again if WaveCount is 10 or less
output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_WaveManagerV2
PARENT: Actor

VAR WaveCount: Int = 1
VAR SpawnDelay: Float = 10.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="Waves Starting!"]
NODE n3: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="DoWave"]
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=WaveCount]
NODE n5: PrintString
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Enemies Spawning"]
NODE n7: GetVar [Variable=SpawnDelay]
NODE n8: Delay
NODE n9: GetVar [Variable=WaveCount]
NODE n10: AddFloat [B=1]
NODE n11: SetVar [Variable=WaveCount]
NODE n12: GetVar [Variable=SpawnDelay]
NODE n13: SubtractFloat [B=1.0]
NODE n14: Clamp [Min=3.0, Max=10.0]
NODE n15: SetVar [Variable=SpawnDelay]
NODE n16: LessThanOrEqual [B=10]
NODE n17: Branch
NODE n18: PrintString [InString="All Waves Done!"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Completed -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Then -> n15.Execute
EXEC n15.Then -> n17.Execute
EXEC n17.True -> n3.Execute
EXEC n17.False -> n18.Execute

DATA n4.Value -> n5.InString [String]
DATA n7.Value -> n8.Duration [Float]
DATA n9.Value -> n10.A [Int]
DATA n10.ReturnValue -> n11.Value [Int]
DATA n12.Value -> n13.A [Float]
DATA n13.ReturnValue -> n14.Value [Float]
DATA n14.ReturnValue -> n15.Value [Float]
DATA n10.ReturnValue -> n16.A [Int]
DATA n16.ReturnValue -> n17.Condition [Bool]

instruction: Create a combat character with Health=100, MaxHealth=100, Armor=10, and IsBlocking boolean. On AnyDamage: if IsBlocking, reduce damage by 50% and print Blocked. Calculate final damage as IncomingDamage minus Armor (minimum 1). Subtract from Health. If Health is at or below zero, print Defeated and destroy. Otherwise print remaining health. Has a custom event ToggleBlock that flips IsBlocking with FlipFlop
output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CombatCharacter
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0
VAR MaxHealth: Float = 100.0
VAR Armor: Float = 10.0
VAR IsBlocking: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=IsBlocking]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: MultiplyFloat [B=0.5]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Blocked!"]
NODE n6: GetVar [Variable=Armor]
NODE n7: SubtractFloat
NODE n8: Clamp [Min=1.0, Max=999.0]
NODE n9: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n10: SubtractFloat
NODE n11: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n12: LessThanOrEqual [B=0.0]
NODE n13: Branch
NODE n14: PrintString [InString="Defeated!"]
NODE n15: DestroyActor
NODE n16: PrintString
NODE n17: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="ToggleBlock"]
NODE n18: FlipFlop
NODE n19: SetVar [Variable=IsBlocking, Value=true]
NODE n20: PrintString [InString="Blocking"]
NODE n21: SetVar [Variable=IsBlocking, Value=false]
NODE n22: PrintString [InString="Not Blocking"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n11.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Then -> n13.Execute
EXEC n13.True -> n14.Execute
EXEC n14.Then -> n15.Execute
EXEC n13.False -> n16.Execute
EXEC n17.Then -> n18.Execute
EXEC n18.A -> n19.Execute
EXEC n19.Then -> n20.Execute
EXEC n18.B -> n21.Execute
EXEC n21.Then -> n22.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]

BLUEPRINT: BP_ReadyCheck
PARENT: Actor
VAR IsReady: Bool = true
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=IsReady]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Go"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Wait"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n3.True -> n4.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n5.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint that on overlap prints Touched and destroys the overlapping actor.

NODE n3: DestroyActor
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
DATA n1.OtherActor -> n3.Target [Actor]

instruction: Create a Blueprint with vectors PointA and PointB and float Alpha. On Tick add DeltaSeconds times 0.3 to Alpha. Lerp between PointA and PointB using Alpha. Set actor location to result.
output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_LerpPlatform
PARENT: Actor
VAR PointA: Vector
VAR PointB: Vector
VAR Alpha: Float = 0.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=Alpha]
NODE n3: MultiplyFloat [B=0.3]
NODE n4: AddFloat
NODE n5: VariableSet [VarName=Alpha]
NODE n6: VariableGet [VarName=PointA]
NODE n7: VariableGet [VarName=PointB]
NODE n8: Lerp
NODE n9: SetActorLocation
EXEC n1.Then -> n5.Execute
DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n2.Value -> n4.A [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.B [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.Value [Float]
EXEC n5.Then -> n9.Execute
DATA n6.Value -> n8.A [Vector]
DATA n7.Value -> n8.B [Vector]
DATA n5.Value -> n8.Alpha [Float]
DATA n8.ReturnValue -> n9.NewLocation [Vector]

instruction: Create a Blueprint with vectors Start and End, float T at 0, and float Dir at 1. On Tick, add DeltaSeconds times Dir to T. If T greater than 1 set Dir to -1. If T less than 0 set Dir to 1. Lerp and set location.
output:

VAR Start: Vector
VAR End: Vector
VAR T: Float = 0.0
VAR Dir: Float = 1.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=T]
NODE n3: VariableGet [VarName=Dir]
NODE n4: MultiplyFloat
NODE n5: AddFloat
NODE n6: VariableSet [VarName=T]
NODE n7: GreaterThan [B=1.0]
NODE n8: Branch
NODE n9: VariableSet [VarName=Dir]
NODE n10: LessThan [B=0.0]
NODE n11: Branch
NODE n12: VariableSet [VarName=Dir]
NODE n13: VariableGet [VarName=Start]
NODE n14: VariableGet [VarName=End]
NODE n15: Lerp
NODE n16: SetActorLocation
EXEC n1.Then -> n6.Execute
DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n4.A [Float]
DATA n3.Value -> n4.B [Float]
DATA n2.Value -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.B [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Float]
EXEC n6.Then -> n8.Execute
DATA n6.Value -> n7.A [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n8.True -> n9.Execute
DATA -1.0 -> n9.Value [Float]
EXEC n9.Then -> n16.Execute
EXEC n8.False -> n11.Execute
DATA n6.Value -> n10.A [Float]
DATA n10.ReturnValue -> n11.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n11.True -> n12.Execute
DATA 1.0 -> n12.Value [Float]
EXEC n12.Then -> n16.Execute
EXEC n11.False -> n16.Execute
DATA n13.Value -> n15.A [Vector]
DATA n14.Value -> n15.B [Vector]
DATA n6.Value -> n15.Alpha [Float]
DATA n15.ReturnValue -> n16.NewLocation [Vector]

NODE n9: SetActorLocation

DATA n7.Value -> n8.B [Vector]

DATA n8.ReturnValue -> n9.NewLocation [Vector]

instruction: Create a Blueprint. On pressing Interact get distance to player pawn. If less than 200, use FlipFlop. A path adds 90 degrees yaw rotation. B path adds -90 degrees yaw rotation.
output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_FlipDoor
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Interact"]
NODE n2: GetPlayerPawn
NODE n3: GetDistanceTo
NODE n4: LessThan [B=200.0]
NODE n5: Branch
NODE n6: FlipFlop
NODE n7: AddActorLocalRotation [DeltaRotation=(Pitch=0,Yaw=90,Roll=0)]
NODE n8: AddActorLocalRotation [DeltaRotation=(Pitch=0,Yaw=-90,Roll=0)]
EXEC n1.Pressed -> n5.Execute
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.OtherActor [Actor]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.A [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n5.True -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.A -> n7.Execute
EXEC n6.B -> n8.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint with bool IsOpen. On pressing Use, check distance to player less than 300. If close, branch on IsOpen. If closed, rotate 90 yaw, set IsOpen true, play sound. If open, rotate -90 yaw, set IsOpen false.
output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SoundDoor
PARENT: Actor
VAR IsOpen: Bool = false
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Use"]
NODE n2: GetPlayerPawn
NODE n3: GetDistanceTo
NODE n4: LessThan [B=300.0]
NODE n5: Branch
NODE n6: VariableGet [VarName=IsOpen]
NODE n7: Branch
NODE n8: AddActorLocalRotation [DeltaRotation=(Pitch=0,Yaw=90,Roll=0)]
NODE n9: VariableSet [VarName=IsOpen]
NODE n10: PlaySoundAtLocation [Sound=DoorOpen]
NODE n11: AddActorLocalRotation [DeltaRotation=(Pitch=0,Yaw=-90,Roll=0)]
NODE n12: VariableSet [VarName=IsOpen]
EXEC n1.Pressed -> n5.Execute
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.OtherActor [Actor]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.A [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n5.True -> n7.Execute
DATA n6.Value -> n7.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n7.False -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute
DATA true -> n9.Value [Bool]
EXEC n9.Then -> n10.Execute
EXEC n7.True -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Then -> n12.Execute
DATA false -> n12.Value [Bool]
